year_opened.note: Opened as Milford Golf Club in 1992. Name history: Milford Golf Club → Milford Paradise Golf Club → The Eagle Milford Golf Club → Hua Hin Seoul Country Club → Hua Hin Korea Golf Club (2016).

green_fee_weekday_thb.note: Everyday flat rate. All-in with caddie and cart: THB 800.

green_fee_weekend_thb.note: Same flat rate applies on weekends.

phone.note: No phone number available; Facebook is primary contact channel.

prose.overview: Hua Hin Korea Golf Club is one of the cheapest 18-hole golf courses in Thailand, offering a flat everyday rate of just 400 THB green fee — or 800 THB all-in with caddie and cart. The course was originally built in 1992 as Milford Golf Club by designer Robert McFarland, measuring 6,614 yards off the back tees at par 72. Over the decades it passed through several name changes — Milford Paradise, The Eagle Milford, Hua Hin Seoul Country Club — before Korean management took over in 2016 and rebranded it as the current Hua Hin Korea Golf Club. Located roughly 40 minutes south of Hua Hin town in Prachuap Khiri Khan province, the course sits in a mountain-framed coastal valley that gives it a scenic setting for the price. For golfers on a strict budget, or those who simply want a casual round without the pressure of a premium venue, this is a viable option — but the maintenance standards are modest, and anyone accustomed to well-manicured conditions should set expectations accordingly.
prose.layout_and_experience: The McFarland layout at what was originally Milford Golf Club uses the natural topography of the valley — mountain ridges to the west, coastal lowlands to the east — to create a course with reasonable visual variety. At 6,614 yards and par 72, the routing is comfortably mid-length, with a mix of par-4s that require straightforward tee-to-green shot-making and par-5s that offer birdie opportunities for longer hitters. The mountain backdrop is genuinely attractive and gives the course a more dramatic feel than the flat rate would suggest. That said, golfers should be prepared for the realities of a low-investment operation: reviewers have noted waterlogged bunkers after rain, patches of thin or unkempt turf on the fairways, and greens that can be slow or uneven. The course is rarely crowded — a genuine benefit that keeps pace of play brisk and the atmosphere relaxed. The Korean management have maintained the operation but have not significantly upgraded the physical infrastructure since taking over. Cart hire is optional, which is unusual for the region.
prose.tips: The best reason to play Hua Hin Korea Golf Club is the price: at 800 THB all-in (green fee, caddie, and cart), it is among the cheapest 18-hole rounds you can play anywhere in Thailand. That value proposition is real, but manage expectations — course conditions are variable and have been described by multiple reviewers as below the standard of other Hua Hin-area clubs. Visit during the November–February dry season to reduce the risk of waterlogged bunkers and soft fairways. Because the course is rarely busy, pace of play is typically fast and you may often play through or ahead of other groups. There is no phone number available; book via the Facebook page or simply show up, as walk-in tee times are generally available. Bring your own water and snacks as on-course F&B facilities are limited. The caddie complement is small, so confirm caddie availability in advance via Facebook if playing in a larger group.
prose.location_and_access: Hua Hin Korea Golf Club is located in Tambon Hua Hin, Prachuap Khiri Khan province, approximately 40 minutes south of Hua Hin town centre by car. The drive from Bangkok covers approximately 255 kilometres via Highway 4 (Phetkasem Road) and takes around 3.5 hours under normal traffic conditions. From Hua Hin town, a taxi or Grab to the course runs roughly 200–300 THB. There is no shuttle service. GPS coordinates 12.4352, 99.9699. The course is not signposted from the main highway, so using the Facebook page map or GPS navigation is advisable.
prose.rental_cta_context: Heading to Hua Hin Korea Golf Club? LENGOLF offers quality rental sets so you can keep your all-in costs low without the hassle of transporting clubs from Bangkok.
